summary: Verified ADR 0279 from the latest realistic `origin/main` baseline at `0.177.112 / 0.130.74`, repaired the Grist persist ownership plus Keycloak and Docker-network recovery paths surfaced during the replay, and completed a successful `r5` live apply that left `grist.example.com` healthy and login-gated on platform version `0.130.75`.

verification.observed: The current replay slice returned `54 passed` across `tests/test_keycloak_runtime_role.py`, `tests/test_grist_runtime_role.py`, `tests/test_grist_playbook.py`, `tests/test_openbao_compose_env_helper.py`, `tests/test_common_docker_bridge_chains_helper.py`, `tests/test_mail_platform_runtime_role.py`, and `tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py`.

`ansible-playbook -i inventory/hosts.yml playbooks/services/grist.yml --syntax-check` passed from the integration worktree before the final replay, and the combined syntax plus focused pytest evidence is preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0279-mainline-validation-r1.txt`.

The March 31 chronology is preserved in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-adr-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply-0.177.113-r1.txt` through `...-r5.txt`; `r3` proved public publication but exposed the missing local login path, `r4` exposed Keycloak bootstrap fragility during shared Docker-network churn, and `r5` succeeded after the Grist persist-ownership repair plus broader startup recovery hardening landed.

The final governed replay in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-adr-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply-0.177.113-r5.txt` ended with play recap `docker-runtime-lv3 : ok=283 changed=2 unreachable=0 failed=0`, `nginx-lv3 : ok=46 changed=5 unreachable=0 failed=0`, and `localhost : ok=24 changed=0 unreachable=0 failed=0`.

verification.check: Current public probes and auth flow
verification.observed: Fresh probes in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0279-mainline-public-probes-r1.txt` show `https://grist.example.com/status` returning the canonical alive string, `https://grist.example.com/o/docs/` returning `HTTP/2 302` into the Keycloak auth flow, and the live discovery issuer still resolving to `https://sso.example.com/realms/lv3`.

Host and guest snapshots in `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0279-mainline-host-state-r1.txt` and `receipts/live-applies/evidence/2026-03-31-ws-0279-mainline-runtime-state-r1.txt` reconfirm kernel `6.17.13-2-pve`, `pve-manager/9.1.6`, running `grist` and `keycloak` containers, `keycloak-keycloak-1` resolving `lv3-mail-stalwart`, `/opt/grist/persist` owned by `1001:1001`, and the split static-plus-secret Grist env contract.
The successful replay still used `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` for this stateful service, and no new standby rehearsal evidence was recorded during the March 31 replay.

notes: Grist first became true on production during the earlier branch-local replay on platform version `0.130.60`; this March 31 receipt records the newer latest-main verification on top of the `0.177.112 / 0.130.74` baseline and the resulting integrated platform baseline advance to `0.130.75`.

The March 31 canonical receipt supersedes the earlier mainline receipt `receipts/live-applies/2026-03-30-adr-0279-grist-mainline-live-apply.json`, while preserving both that receipt and the original branch-local receipt as part of the audit trail.

The successful replay was executed from the `ws-0279-main-merge-r2` worktree built on top of `origin/main` commit `58dad5d37ae16ceb3a73bc2fe4554b2a449e8f83`; the final merge preserves those same runtime and evidence surfaces.
